sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports pain in the left shoulder, describing it as sharp and constant. He states the pain began four days ago following a fall while playing football. The patient also reports a headache and reports pain in the left hand. He denies fever and numbness in his fingers. The patient is currently taking Tylenol for the pain.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure is 118/72 mmHg. Heart rate is 78 bpm. Temperature is 98.6°F. Neurological exam reveals normal strength and sensation in all extremities. Range of motion in the left shoulder is limited due to pain.
medical_concepts: limited range of motion of left shoulder

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's symptoms are consistent with a shoulder injury and possible fracture. He has a history of football participation and a previous appendectomy.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Order X-rays of the left shoulder to evaluate for fracture. Recommend avoiding tackle football to prevent further injury. Schedule follow-up in one week to assess healing and pain management.

medical_concepts: limited range of motion of left shoulder
